title: StatusUpdateDraft
description: Output of Agent: status-update-author. A weekly (or bounded-period) project status update drafted from operational data. Extends pm_artifact_base — same top-level shape, with metadata.* tightened to the status update sections.

project_id.description: Project key (e.g. 'DC-OH-08' / 'QPB1'). Drives Drive folder routing and search index partition.

period_start.description: Inclusive start date (YYYY-MM-DD) of the reporting window.

period_end.description: Inclusive end date (YYYY-MM-DD) of the reporting window.

audience.description: Drives tone, depth of cost detail, and Lane: 'owner' forces Lane 3 (Charles approves before send), 'partner' is Lane 2, 'internal' Lane 2 default.

headline_status.description: Overall project health for the period.

data_freshness.description: Recency timestamps per source. If any are older than the period_end by >48h, the agent should flag 'data_freshness_stale' in escalation_reasons.

sections.description: Structured section text. body_markdown is the rendered concatenation; these fields keep the parts addressable for downstream re-use (e.g. owner deck slides).

executive_summary.description: 3–6 sentence narrative for an owner exec. Plain English, no jargon, no fluff.

schedule.description: Schedule narrative: critical-path status, milestones hit/missed in period, look-ahead deltas vs baseline.

cost.description: Cost narrative: committed vs. forecast, change orders in period, contingency drawdown. Numbers must be cited.

safety.description: Safety narrative: incidents (or 'no recordables'), TRIR, leading indicators, observations.

rfis_submittals.description: RFI/submittal flow: opened, closed, oldest open, anything blocking.

procurement.description: Long-lead status, deliveries this period, expediting issues.

risks.description: Top risks for the period; each risk should appear in body_markdown with owner + mitigation.

look_ahead.description: Next 1–2 weeks: milestones, deliveries, decisions needed, asks of the audience.

metrics.description: Optional structured metrics. Numbers shown in body_markdown SHOULD also appear here so downstream tooling can re-render without re-parsing prose.

cost_variance_pct.description: Forecast variance vs. baseline as a percent. Negative = under, positive = over.
